q1.prompt: While most DNA viruses replicate their genomes within the host cell nucleus, one family of large, complex DNA viruses is unique because its members replicate entirely within the host cell cytoplasm. Which of the following is an example of such a virus?

options.label: Herpesviridae
Adenoviridae
Papillomaviridae
Poxviridae
Hepadnaviridae

q2.prompt: Naked capsid viruses, such as Norovirus and Hepatitis A virus, are commonly transmitted via the fecal-oral route. Which structural property of these viruses primarily allows them to survive transit through the stomach and remain infectious despite exposure to detergents and acidic pH?

options.label: A lipid-rich lipoprotein envelope derived from host membranes
A complex, pleomorphic outer coat with attachment fiber proteins
A stable, proteinaceous icosahedral capsid
A helical nucleocapsid symmetry that resists environmental drying
The presence of a dense, protein-rich tegument layer

q3.prompt: A laboratory is using electron microscopy to identify a virus isolated from a patient who died of a progressive neurological infection following a bat bite. The virions are observed to have a highly characteristic morphology associated with the Rhabdoviridae family. How are these virions described?

options.label: Brick-shaped with complex internal filaments
Long, filamentous threads with a hooked end
Icosahedral with wheel-like spokes on the capsid
Bullet-shaped with a helical nucleocapsid
Pleomorphic and spherical with large surface spikes

q4.prompt: Influenza A viruses utilize specific surface glycoproteins to facilitate their replication cycle. Which specific role is performed by the Hemagglutinin (HA) protein during the initial stages of infection?

options.label: Cleavage of sialic acid residues to allow the release of progeny virions
Formation of a transmembrane proton channel for genomic uncoating
Binding to cell-surface sialic acid receptors to facilitate viral attachment
Synthesis of a positive-strand mRNA template from the negative-strand genome
Proteolytic cleavage of the viral polyprotein precursor

q5.prompt: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) is classified as a "dsDNA RT" virus because its replication strategy differs significantly from most other DNA viruses. Which specific enzymatic process is utilized by HBV to produce its progeny DNA genome?

options.label: Utilization of host cell DNA polymerase for semi-conservative replication
Direct translation of the DNA genome by host cell ribosomes in the cytoplasm
Use of reverse transcriptase to synthesize DNA from an RNA intermediate
Integration of the circular double-stranded DNA genome into host mitochondria
Semiconservative replication occurring solely within the host cytoplasm

q6.prompt: Rotavirus is a major cause of severe infantile gastroenteritis worldwide. Its genome has a specific structure that permits the exchange of genetic material between different strains (reassortment), potentially leading to significant antigenic variation. How is the Rotavirus genome structured?

options.label: A single-stranded, nonsegmented positive-sense RNA genome
A double-stranded, segmented RNA genome
A partially double-stranded circular DNA genome
A single-stranded, linear DNA genome
A diploid positive-sense RNA genome

q7.prompt: A 19-year-old student presents with fever, pharyngitis, and cervical lymphadenopathy. The clinician suspects infectious mononucleosis caused by the Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV). Which laboratory test is specifically used to detect the heterophile antibodies associated with this acute infection?

options.label: Tzanck smear for multinucleated giant cells
Monospot test
Quellung reaction for capsular swelling
Southern blot for viral integration sites
Darkfield microscopy for spiral organisms

q8.prompt: Cytomegalovirus (CMV) is a significant cause of congenital infection when primary infection occurs during pregnancy. Which of the following clinical findings is part of the classic presentation of a neonate with symptomatic congenital CMV infection?

options.label: Aplastic crisis in the setting of chronic hemolytic anemia
Barking cough and inspiratory stridor due to subglottic swelling
Microcephaly, sensorineural deafness, and a "blueberry muffin" rash
High fever followed by the sudden appearance of a maculopapular truncal rash
Acute hemorrhagic cystitis and epidemic keratoconjunctivitis

q9.prompt: The Rabies virus enters the host via an animal bite and must gain entry into peripheral neurons to travel to the central nervous system. Which host cell receptor is utilized by the virus to initiate this entry process?

options.label: CD4 glycoprotein on helper T cells
Intercellular adhesion molecule-1 (ICAM-1)
Nicotinic acetylcholine receptor
P antigen (globoside) on erythroid precursors
ACE2 receptor on respiratory epithelial cells

q10.prompt: A patient's Hepatitis B serology panel shows the following results: Negative HBsAg, negative anti-HBs, and positive IgM anti-HBc. These results are characteristic of which clinical stage of Hepatitis B infection?

options.label: The "window period" of an acute infection
Early acute infection with high levels of viral replication
Immunity resulting from a successful vaccination series
Chronic carrier state with low infectivity
Complete recovery and immunity following a prior natural infection
